@description.name: @description
@description.body: This validation manager is responsible for validation of forms.

@description.name: @description
@description.body: Change event for the valid state.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Signals that the validation is done. This is not needed on synchronous
validation (validation is done right after the call) but very important
in the case an asynchronous validator will be used.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
The validator of the form itself. You can set a function (for
synchronous validation) or a {@link qx.ui.form.validation.AsyncValidator}.
In both cases, the function can have all added form items as first
argument and the manager as a second argument. The manager should be used
to set the {@link #invalidMessage}.

Keep in mind that the validator is optional if you don't need the
validation in the context of the whole form.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
The invalid message should store the message why the form validation
failed. It will be added to the array returned by
{@link #getInvalidMessages}.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
This message will be shown if a required field is empty and no individual
{@link qx.ui.form.MForm#requiredInvalidMessage} is given.

@description.name: @description
@description.body: The context for the form validation.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Add a form item to the validation manager. The form item has to implement at least two interfaces:
  1. The {@link qx.ui.form.IForm} Interface
  2. One of the following interfaces:
    • {@link qx.ui.form.IBooleanForm}
    • {@link qx.ui.form.IColorForm}
    • {@link qx.ui.form.IDateForm}
    • {@link qx.ui.form.INumberForm}
    • {@link qx.ui.form.IStringForm}
The validator can be a synchronous or asynchronous validator. In both cases the validator can either returns a boolean or fire an {@link qx.core.ValidationError}. For synchronous validation, a plain JavaScript function should be used. For all asynchronous validations, a {@link qx.ui.form.validation.AsyncValidator} is needed to wrap the plain function.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: formItem {qx.ui.core.Widget} The form item to add.
@param.description: The form item to add.

@param.name: @param
@param.body:
validator {Function | qx.ui.form.validation.AsyncValidator}
  The validator.
@param.description: The validator.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: context {var?null} The context of the validator.
@param.description: The context of the validator.

@description.name: @description
@description.body: Remove a form item from the validation manager.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: formItem {qx.ui.core.Widget} The form item to remove.
@param.description: The form item to remove.

@return.name: @return
@return.body: {qx.ui.core.Widget?null} The removed form item or null if the item could not be found.
@return.description: The removed form item or null if the item could not be found.

@description.name: @description
@description.body: Returns registered form items from the validation manager.

@return.name: @return
@return.body: {Array} The form items which will be validated.
@return.description: The form items which will be validated.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Invokes the validation. If only synchronous validators are set, the
result of the whole validation is available at the end of the method
and can be returned. If an asynchronous validator is set, the result
is still unknown at the end of this method so nothing will be returned.
In both cases, a {@link #complete} event will be fired if the validation
has ended. The result of the validation can then be accessed with the
{@link #getValid} method.

@return.name: @return
@return.body: {Boolean|undefined} The validation result, if available.
@return.description: The validation result, if available.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Checks if the form item is required. If so, the value is checked
and the result will be returned. If the form item is not required, true
will be returned.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: formItem {qx.ui.core.Widget} The form item to check.
@param.description: The form item to check.

@return.name: @return
@return.body: {var} Validation result
@return.description: Validation result

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Validates a form item. This method handles the differences of
synchronous and asynchronous validation and returns the result of the
validation if possible (synchronous cases). If the validation is
asynchronous, null will be returned.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: dataEntry {Object} The map stored in {@link #add}
@param.description: The map stored in {@link #add}

@param.name: @param
@param.body: value {var} The currently set value
@param.description: The currently set value

@return.name: @return
@return.body: {Boolean|null} Validation result or null for async validation
@return.description: Validation result or null for async validation

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Validates the form. It checks for asynchronous validation and handles
the differences to synchronous validation. If no form validator is given,
true will be returned. If a synchronous validator is given, the
validation result will be returned. In asynchronous cases, null will be
returned cause the result is not available.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: items {qx.ui.core.Widget[]} An array of all form items.
@param.description: An array of all form items.

@return.name: @return
@return.body: {Boolean|null} description
@return.description: description

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Helper function which checks, if the given validator is synchronous
or asynchronous.

@param.name: @param
@param.body:
validator {Function|qx.ui.form.validation.AsyncValidator}
  The validator to check.
@param.description: The validator to check.

@return.name: @return
@return.body: {Boolean} True, if the given validator is asynchronous.
@return.description: True, if the given validator is asynchronous.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Returns true, if the given item implements the {@link qx.ui.form.IForm}
interface.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: formItem {qx.core.Object} The item to check.
@param.description: The item to check.

@return.name: @return
@return.body:
{Boolean} true, if the given item implements the
  necessary interface.
@return.description:
true, if the given item implements the
  necessary interface.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Returns true, if the given item implements the
{@link qx.ui.core.ISingleSelection} interface.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: formItem {qx.core.Object} The item to check.
@param.description: The item to check.

@return.name: @return
@return.body:
{Boolean} true, if the given item implements the
  necessary interface.
@return.description:
true, if the given item implements the
  necessary interface.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Returns true, if the given item implements the
{@link qx.data.controller.ISelection} interface.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: formItem {qx.core.Object} The item to check.
@param.description: The item to check.

@return.name: @return
@return.body:
{Boolean} true, if the given item implements the
  necessary interface.
@return.description:
true, if the given item implements the
  necessary interface.

Sets the valid state of the manager. It generates the event if
necessary and stores the new value.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: value {Boolean|null} The new valid state of the manager.

@description.body:
Responsible for showing a tooltip in case the validation is done for
widgets based on qx.ui.core.Widget.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: valid {Boolean} false, if the tooltip should be shown
@param.description: false, if the tooltip should be shown

@return.description: The valid state of the manager.

@return.body: {Boolean|null} The valid state of the manager.
@return.description: The valid state of the manager.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Returns an array of all invalid messages of the invalid form items and
the form manager itself.

@return.name: @return

@return.description: All invalid messages.

@description.name: @description
@description.body: Resets the validator.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: prop {String} Name of the property.
@param.description: Name of the property.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Internal helper method to set the given item to valid for asynchronous
validation calls. This indirection is used to determinate if the
validation process is completed or if other asynchronous validators
are still validating. {@link #__checkValidationComplete} checks if the
validation is complete and will be called at the end of this method.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: formItem {qx.ui.core.Widget} The form item to set the valid state.
@param.description: The form item to set the valid state.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: valid {Boolean} The valid state for the form item.
@param.description: The valid state for the form item.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Internal helper method to set the form manager to valid for asynchronous
validation calls. This indirection is used to determinate if the
validation process is completed or if other asynchronous validators
are still validating. {@link #__checkValidationComplete} checks if the
validation is complete and will be called at the end of this method.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: valid {Boolean} The valid state for the form manager.
@param.description: The valid state for the form manager.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Checks if all asynchronous validators have validated so the result
is final and the {@link #complete} event can be fired. If that's not
the case, nothing will happen in the method.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
The bind method delegates the call to the
{@link qx.data.SingleValueBinding#bind} function. As source, the current
object (this) will be used.

@param.name: @param
@param.body:
sourcePropertyChain {String} The property chain which represents
  the source property.
@param.description:
The property chain which represents
  the source property.

@param.name: @param
@param.body:
targetObject {qx.core.Object} The object which the source should
  be bind to.
@param.description:
The object which the source should
  be bind to.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: targetProperty {String} The property name of the target object.
@param.description: The property name of the target object.

@param.name: @param
@param.body:
options {Map?null} A map containing the options. See
  {@link qx.data.SingleValueBinding#bind} for more
  information.
@param.description:
A map containing the options. See
  {@link qx.data.SingleValueBinding#bind} for more
  information.

@return.name: @return
@return.body:
{var} Returns the internal id for that binding. This can be used
  for referencing the binding e.g. for removing. This is not an atomic
  id so you can't you use it as a hash-map index.
@return.description:
Returns the internal id for that binding. This can be used
  for referencing the binding e.g. for removing. This is not an atomic
  id so you can't you use it as a hash-map index.

@throws.name: @throws
@throws.body:
{qx.core.AssertionError} If the event is no data event or
  there is no property definition for object and property (source and
  target).
@throws.description:
If the event is no data event or
  there is no property definition for object and property (source and
  target).

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
The bind method delegates the call to the
{@link qx.data.SingleValueBinding#bind} function. As source, the current
object (this) will be used.

@param.name: @param
@param.body:
sourcePropertyChain {String} The property chain which represents
  the source property.
@param.description:
The property chain which represents
  the source property.

@param.name: @param
@param.body:
targetObject {qx.core.Object} The object which the source should
  be bind to.
@param.description:
The object which the source should
  be bind to.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: targetProperty {String} The property name of the target object.
@param.description: The property name of the target object.

@param.name: @param
@param.body:
options {Map} A map containing the options. See
  {@link qx.data.SingleValueBinding#bind} for more
  information.
@param.description:
A map containing the options. See
  {@link qx.data.SingleValueBinding#bind} for more
  information.

@return.name: @return
@return.body:
{qx.Promise} A promise which is resolved when the initial value
	 has been set on the target.  Note that this does NOT resolve when subsequent
  values are returned.  The promise value is the internal id for that binding.
  The id can be used for referencing the binding e.g. for removing. This is not
  an atomic id so you can't you use it as a hash-map index.
@return.description:
A promise which is resolved when the initial value
	 has been set on the target.  Note that this does NOT resolve when subsequent
  values are returned.  The promise value is the internal id for that binding.
  The id can be used for referencing the binding e.g. for removing. This is not
  an atomic id so you can't you use it as a hash-map index.

@throws.name: @throws
@throws.body:
{qx.core.AssertionError} If the event is no data event or
  there is no property definition for object and property (source and
  target).
@throws.description:
If the event is no data event or
  there is no property definition for object and property (source and
  target).

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Removes the binding with the given id from the current object. The
id has to be the id returned by any of the bind functions.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: id {var} The id of the binding.
@param.description: The id of the binding.

@throws.name: @throws
@throws.body: {Error} If the binding could not be found.
@throws.description: If the binding could not be found.

@description.name: @description
@description.body: Removes all bindings between the object and the related one.

@param.name: @param
@param.body:
relatedObject {qx.core.Object} The object of which related
  bindings should be removed.
@param.description:
The object of which related
  bindings should be removed.

@throws.name: @throws
@throws.body:
{Error} If one of the bindings listed internally can not be
  removed.
@throws.description:
If one of the bindings listed internally can not be
  removed.

@description.name: @description
@description.body: Returns an array which lists all bindings for the object.

@return.name: @return
@return.body:
{Array} An array of binding informations. Every binding
  information is an array itself containing id, sourceObject, sourceEvent,
  targetObject and targetProperty in that order.
@return.description:
An array of binding informations. Every binding
  information is an array itself containing id, sourceObject, sourceEvent,
  targetObject and targetProperty in that order.

Helper that calls the appropriate logger function with the current object
and any number of items.

@description.name: @description
@description.body: Add event listener to this object.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: type {String} name of the event type
@param.description: name of the event type

@param.name: @param
@param.body: listener {Function} event callback function
@param.description: event callback function

@param.name: @param
@param.body:
self {Object ? null} Reference to the 'this' variable inside
        the event listener. When not given, the corresponding dispatcher
        usually falls back to a default, which is the target
        by convention. Note this is not a strict requirement, i.e.
        custom dispatchers can follow a different strategy.
@param.description:
Reference to the 'this' variable inside
        the event listener. When not given, the corresponding dispatcher
        usually falls back to a default, which is the target
        by convention. Note this is not a strict requirement, i.e.
        custom dispatchers can follow a different strategy.

@param.name: @param
@param.body:
capture {Boolean ? false} Whether to attach the event to the
        capturing phase or the bubbling phase of the event. The default is
        to attach the event handler to the bubbling phase.
@param.description:
Whether to attach the event to the
        capturing phase or the bubbling phase of the event. The default is
        to attach the event handler to the bubbling phase.

@return.name: @return
@return.body:
{String} An opaque id, which can be used to remove the event listener
        using the {@link #removeListenerById} method.
@return.description:
An opaque id, which can be used to remove the event listener
        using the {@link #removeListenerById} method.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Add event listener to this object, which is only called once. After the
listener is called the event listener gets removed.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: type {String} name of the event type
@param.description: name of the event type

@param.name: @param
@param.body: listener {Function} event callback function
@param.description: event callback function

@param.name: @param
@param.body: context {Object ? window} reference to the 'this' variable inside the callback
@param.description: reference to the 'this' variable inside the callback

@param.name: @param
@param.body:
capture {Boolean ? false} Whether to attach the event to the
        capturing phase or the bubbling phase of the event. The default is
        to attach the event handler to the bubbling phase.
@param.description:
Whether to attach the event to the
        capturing phase or the bubbling phase of the event. The default is
        to attach the event handler to the bubbling phase.

@return.name: @return
@return.body:
{String} An opaque id, which can be used to remove the event listener
        using the {@link #removeListenerById} method.
@return.description:
An opaque id, which can be used to remove the event listener
        using the {@link #removeListenerById} method.

@description.name: @description

@param.description: name of the event type

@param.description: event callback function

@param.name: @param
@param.body: self {Object ? null} reference to the 'this' variable inside the callback

@param.name: @param
@param.body:
capture {Boolean} Whether to remove the event listener of
  the bubbling or of the capturing phase.
@param.description:
Whether to remove the event listener of
  the bubbling or of the capturing phase.

@return.name: @return
@return.body: {Boolean} Whether the event was removed successfully (has existed)
@return.description: Whether the event was removed successfully (has existed)

Whether to check for listeners of
        the bubbling or of the capturing phase.

@return.name: @return

@return.description: Whether the object has a listener of the given type.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Create an event object and dispatch it on this object.
The event dispatched with this method does never bubble! Use only if you
are sure that bubbling is not required.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: type {String} Event type to fire
@param.description: Event type to fire

@param.name: @param
@param.body: clazz {Class?qx.event.type.Event} The event class
@param.description: The event class

@param.name: @param
@param.body:
args {Array?null} Arguments, which will be passed to
      the event's init method.
@param.description:
Arguments, which will be passed to
      the event's init method.

@return.name: @return
@return.body:
{Boolean} Whether the event default was prevented or not.
    Returns true, when the event was NOT prevented.
@return.description:
Whether the event default was prevented or not.
    Returns true, when the event was NOT prevented.

@description.name: @description
@description.body: Creates and dispatches an non-bubbling data event on this object.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: type {String} Event type to fire
@param.description: Event type to fire

@param.name: @param
@param.body: data {var} User defined data attached to the event object
@param.description: User defined data attached to the event object

@param.name: @param
@param.body: oldData {var?null} The event's old data (optional)
@param.description: The event's old data (optional)

@param.name: @param
@param.body:
cancelable {Boolean?false} Whether or not an event can have its default
    action prevented. The default action can either be the browser's
    default action of a native event (e.g. open the context menu on a
    right click) or the default action of a qooxdoo class (e.g. close
    the window widget). The default action can be prevented by calling
    {@link qx.event.type.Event#preventDefault}
@param.description:
Whether or not an event can have its default
    action prevented. The default action can either be the browser's
    default action of a native event (e.g. open the context menu on a
    right click) or the default action of a qooxdoo class (e.g. close
    the window widget). The default action can be prevented by calling
    {@link qx.event.type.Event#preventDefault}

@return.name: @return
@return.body:
{Boolean} Whether the event default was prevented or not.
    Returns true, when the event was NOT prevented.
@return.description:
Whether the event default was prevented or not.
    Returns true, when the event was NOT prevented.

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Sets multiple properties at once by using a property list or
sets one property and its value by the first and second argument.
As a fallback, if no generated property setter could be found, a
handwritten setter will be searched and invoked if available.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: data {Map | String} a map of property values. The key is the name of the property.
@param.description: a map of property values. The key is the name of the property.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: value {var?} the value, only used when data is a string.
@param.description: the value, only used when data is a string.

@return.name: @return

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Creates the object and adds it to a list; most classes are expected to
override `_createQxObjectImpl` NOT this method.

@param.body: id {String} ID of the object
@param.description: ID of the object

@return.body: {qx.core.Object?} the created object

@description.name: @description
@description.body:
Creates the object, intended to be overridden. Null is a valid return
value and will be cached by `getQxObject`, however `undefined` is NOT a
valid value and so will not be cached meaning that `_createQxObjectImpl`
will be called multiple times until a valid value is returned.

@param.name: @param
@param.body: id {String} ID of the object
@param.description: ID of the object

@return.name: @return
@return.body: {qx.core.Object?} the created object
@return.description: the created object
